elements.sent-2411-001: Free Steiner triple systems (STS) are infinite structures that are naturally characterised by a universal property.
elements.sent-2411-002: We consider the class of free STSs from a model theoretic viewpoint.
elements.sent-2411-003: We show that free STSs on any number of generators are elementarily equivalent.
elements.sent-2411-004: We axiomatise their theory and show that it is stable.
elements.sent-2411-005: The subject of this paper is the first-order theory of a class of combinatorial structures called free Steiner triple systems.
elements.sent-2411-006: A Steiner triple system (STS) of order $\lambda$ is a pair $(M, \mathcal{B )$ where $M$ is a set with $\lambda$ elements, and $\mathcal{B}$ is a collection of 3-element subsets of $M$ (the blocks) such that any two $x, y \in M$ are contained in exactly one block.}
elements.sent-2411-007: A set $M$ with a collection of 3-element subsets is a partial STS (PSTS) if any two elements of $M$ belong to at most one block.
elements.sent-2411-008: Steiner triple systems are well known combinatorial structures, widely studied in the finite case.
elements.sent-2411-009: Recently, certain infinite incarnations have appeared in the model-theoretic literature, e.g. as examples of strongly minimal structures \cite{bald22 , or as specific constructions such as Fra\"{\i ss\'e amalgamations \cite{barcas .}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: A notable construction method, that also applies to incidence structures and, more generally, to algebras, generates free Steiner triple systems.
elements.sent-2411-011: Universal algebra gives a standard characterization of free constructions, which captures the intuition that an infinite STS can be obtained from an initial set $A$ containing $\lambda$ vertices with no blocks defined among them by adding, for each pair of vertices $a_1, a_2 \in A$, a new point that gives the unique block to which $a_1$ and $a_2$ belong.
elements.sent-2411-012: Iterating this process $\omega$ times gives an STS.
elements.sent-2411-013: The template example in \cite{chicotetal is, essentially, the result of this construction for $\lambda=3$.}
elements.sent-2411-014: Standard universal-algebraic tools tell us that such constructions are uniquely determined up to isomorphism by the cardinality of the set $A$.

elements.sent-2411-019: The relation $R$ is interpreted as the set of blocks: three elements $x, y$ and $z$ are in a block if and only if $R(x,y,z)$.
elements.sent-2411-020: With this choice of language, a substructure of an STS is a partial STS.
